title: Add viewport safe-area and dynamic viewport handling

analysis.summary: US-028 already established the foundation: viewport-fit=cover, 100dvh/100dvw with fallbacks, visualViewport API integration, and safe-area-inset on bottom/left/right of buttons. US-037 closes remaining gaps: safe-area-inset-top coverage, info overlay safe-area padding, overscroll prevention, touch-action hardening, and landscape notch handling.
existing_infrastructure: visualViewport API resize listener in resize.ts
gaps_to_address: No safe-area-inset-top coverage — notch/dynamic island could overlap top of overlay content
Info overlay (.eavi-info-overlay) has no safe-area padding for its content area
No overscroll-behavior: none — mobile can trigger pull-to-refresh or rubber-band bounce
No touch-action constraint on canvas/body to prevent unintended browser gestures
Landscape orientation with notch: buttons and overlay content could be clipped on left/right sides
Info panel close button (.eavi-info-close) could be hidden behind notch in landscape

changes: Add overscroll-behavior: none to html,body to prevent pull-to-refresh and rubber-banding
Add touch-action: none to #app and canvas to prevent browser gesture interference
Add safe-area padding to .eavi-info-overlay so overlay content respects all four insets
Add safe-area-aware padding to .eavi-info-panel for landscape notch scenarios
No changes needed — viewport-fit=cover already present
No changes needed — visualViewport integration and orientation handling already complete

files_to_create.purpose: Test CSS rules for US-037 acceptance criteria: safe-area insets on overlay, overscroll-behavior, touch-action, no scrollbars

details: T-037-02: #app or canvas includes touch-action: none
T-037-03: .eavi-info-overlay uses safe-area-inset padding (all four sides)
T-037-04: .eavi-info-panel accounts for safe-area in padding
T-037-05: index.html viewport meta includes viewport-fit=cover (re-verify from US-028)
T-037-06: html,body overflow is hidden (re-verify no scrollbars)
T-037-07: html,body uses 100dvh with 100vh fallback (re-verify dynamic viewport)
Add overscroll-behavior: none after overflow: hidden to prevent pull-to-refresh and rubber-band bounce on mobile
Add touch-action: none to prevent unintended pan/zoom gestures that could cause blank bars or scrollbars
Add padding using env(safe-area-inset-*) so overlay content does not render behind notch, dynamic island, or home indicator
Run npm run build to confirm no build errors
Run npx vitest run tests/visual/viewport-safe-area.test.ts to confirm new tests pass
Run full test suite to confirm no regressions

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Viewport meta config supports edge-to-ed: Already satisfied by viewport-fit=cover in index.html (US-028). Verified in step 1 test T-037-05.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.UI respects safe-area insets (top/bottom: Steps 2-4: buttons already have bottom/side insets (US-028); overlay gains all-four-side safe-area padding.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Dynamic viewport height changes do not l: Already satisfied by 100dvh + visualViewport listener (US-028). Reinforced by overscroll-behavior: none (step 2) and touch-action: none (step 3).
acceptance_criteria_mapping.No scrollbars appear: Already satisfied by overflow: hidden (US-028). Reinforced by overscroll-behavior: none and touch-action: none.

low_risk: overscroll-behavior: none is well-supported and purely additive
Safe-area padding on overlay is purely additive
All changes are CSS-only — no JS runtime changes needed
medium_risk: touch-action: none on #app may interfere with pointer entropy collection if pointer.ts relies on touch events — verify pointer.ts uses pointer events (not touch events) which are unaffected by touch-action
risk_assessment.mitigation: Verify src/input/pointer.ts uses pointermove/pointerdown (not touchmove/touchstart). Pointer events fire regardless of touch-action: none.

notes: This is primarily a CSS-only ticket. The heavy lifting (visualViewport, dvh, viewport-fit=cover) was done in US-028. US-037 hardens the remaining edge cases: overlay safe-area coverage, overscroll prevention, and gesture interference.
